snapshot.scope: Independent Planning review binds the External Managed Copy Backup implementation and safety repairs to the backup, source-storage, Job, settings, architecture, reliability and trace owners on origin/main 95b62cb.

reviewers.task: External managed-copy backup and restore contract-impact review
reviewers.independence: Product Planning did not author the Backup implementation and independently compared the exact stable-ID, binding, archive, restore, Job-recovery and disclosure behavior with owner contracts and trace evidence before binding this snapshot.

dimensions.summary: Existing Data, Source Storage, Job, Settings, Architecture and Reliability owners carry the complete backup/restore behavior without introducing a duplicate owner document or speculative UI owner.

Owner text agrees that reachable Pige-managed copies are included, absolute bindings stay machine-local, missing or rebound roots wait durably, and restore rewrites included copies into vault ownership without false reconnect warnings.

New exact test evidence narrows the Backup Requirement and E9.01 gaps without promotion; semantic claims retain partial status and enumerate reconnect, incomplete-control, platform and coordinator residuals.

Focused regressions identify inclusion, archive disclosure, binding and file races, durable reconnect recovery, deterministic in-vault restore mapping and truthful dependency counts while preserving current service boundaries.

No new owner document or durable Decision was introduced; existing subject owners, two bounded evidence entries and this review recipe carry the implementation delta without duplicating the backup policy.

verification.command: Independent implementation and owner-contract review of External Managed Copy Backup based on origin/main 95b62cb
verification.evidence: Review covers Main backup/coordinator implementation, executable schema, focused tests and every affected owner/trace projection; raw machine bindings and referenced-original payloads remain excluded.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: Links, whitespace, document map, PRD structure, decision records and narrowed Backup requirement/evidence mappings pass after the bounded owner synchronization.

verification.command: npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:independent-documentation-review && npm run verify:documentation-leanness
verification.evidence: CON-001 through CON-004 remain green and the current recipe binds all governed roots plus the changed Backup code/tests without Requirement, Exit or Phase promotion.

coordination.compatibility: The candidate implements the existing default-inclusion and portable-restore policy additively; legacy manifests remain readable, referenced originals stay excluded, and no release scope, Requirement, Exit or Phase status changes.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning synchronized existing data, source, Job, settings, architecture, reliability and trace owners; this review binds current implementation without claiming unavailable reconnect or incomplete-backup controls.

nonBlockingRisks: No renderer-safe external-root chooser/reconnect owner exists; the durable waiting_dependency is body-free but the current Settings surface cannot complete repair in place.
No explicit continue-incomplete flow exists; a required missing or rebound managed-copy root therefore blocks publication rather than silently omitting Pige-owned evidence.
BackupCoordinatorService retains its specialized Job transition/checkpoint owner pending continuous migration into the generic JobExecutionCoordinator.
Windows and signed packaged backup/restore evidence remain open, including cross-platform path identity and recovery behavior.

Broader complete memory/conversation fixtures, release-wide exclusion scans, richer Operation facts and final syscall/cross-file atomicity remain open; no Requirement, Exit or Phase status is promoted.
